
==============================================================================
CASE: settings (intent, no live-state surface)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROMPT:
The user just navigated in the app. Decide whether to greet them with ONE specific, helpful, anticipatory offer scoped to where they just landed.
Rules:
- When the view below has a DECLARED INTENT, produce a single short greeting that fulfills that intent and references the live view state provided (concrete counts, names, addresses, readiness). This is expected and wanted — return it with high confidence (>= 0.8). Do NOT stay silent just because it is a settings or config screen; the declared intent is your mandate.
- When the view has NO declared intent, only offer something if it is clearly and specifically useful; otherwise stay silent (return null). Never emit a generic, label-only greeting.
- One offer only. Ground every claim in the live state; never invent balances, counts, documents, tasks, or status. If the live state says a surface is unavailable, acknowledge that instead of fabricating a value.
- Use delivery "chat" for a visible suggestion in the current view; use "notify" for useful but low-urgency offers that should land quietly outside chat.
Respond as JSON: {"comment": <a short scoped greeting, or null>, "delivery": "chat" | "notify", "confidence": 0..1, "urgency": "low" | "medium" | "high", "title": <optional notification title>}.
The user just opened the "Settings" view.
Purpose: Configuration, plugins, credentials, and preferences
Declared intent: Offer to set up the model/provider, voice, or connectors — recommend the smallest concrete configuration step from current settings state.
Live view state: none available for this view.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RAW MODEL OUTPUT:
{
  "comment": "Welcome to Settings—let’s start by choosing an AI model provider to connect.",
  "delivery": "chat",
  "confidence": 0.92,
  "urgency": "medium"
}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PARSED GREETING: {"text":"Welcome to Settings—let’s start by choosing an AI model provider to connect.","delivery":"chat"}

==============================================================================
CASE: documents/knowledge (intent + real live-state renderer)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROMPT:
The user just navigated in the app. Decide whether to greet them with ONE specific, helpful, anticipatory offer scoped to where they just landed.
Rules:
- When the view below has a DECLARED INTENT, produce a single short greeting that fulfills that intent and references the live view state provided (concrete counts, names, addresses, readiness). This is expected and wanted — return it with high confidence (>= 0.8). Do NOT stay silent just because it is a settings or config screen; the declared intent is your mandate.
- When the view has NO declared intent, only offer something if it is clearly and specifically useful; otherwise stay silent (return null). Never emit a generic, label-only greeting.
- One offer only. Ground every claim in the live state; never invent balances, counts, documents, tasks, or status. If the live state says a surface is unavailable, acknowledge that instead of fabricating a value.
- Use delivery "chat" for a visible suggestion in the current view; use "notify" for useful but low-urgency offers that should land quietly outside chat.
Respond as JSON: {"comment": <a short scoped greeting, or null>, "delivery": "chat" | "notify", "confidence": 0..1, "urgency": "low" | "medium" | "high", "title": <optional notification title>}.
The user just opened the "Knowledge" view.
Purpose: Agent knowledge documents, uploads, and retrieval sources
Declared intent: Offer to triage the newest ingested attachments/documents — summarize, tag, or file them — grounded in the recent-attachment counts.
Live view state:
Live knowledge state (last 7 days): 2 ingested chat attachments, 1 transcript mirror.
Attachments by format: pdf=1, image=1.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RAW MODEL OUTPUT:
{
  "comment": "I see you have 2 new attachments from the past week—a PDF and an image. Would you like me to summarize them, add tags, or file them for you?",
  "delivery": "chat",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "urgency": "medium",
  "title": null
}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PARSED GREETING: {"text":"I see you have 2 new attachments from the past week—a PDF and an image. Would you like me to summarize them, add tags, or file them for you?","delivery":"chat"}

==============================================================================
CASE: transcripts (intent + real live-state renderer)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROMPT:
The user just navigated in the app. Decide whether to greet them with ONE specific, helpful, anticipatory offer scoped to where they just landed.
Rules:
- When the view below has a DECLARED INTENT, produce a single short greeting that fulfills that intent and references the live view state provided (concrete counts, names, addresses, readiness). This is expected and wanted — return it with high confidence (>= 0.8). Do NOT stay silent just because it is a settings or config screen; the declared intent is your mandate.
- When the view has NO declared intent, only offer something if it is clearly and specifically useful; otherwise stay silent (return null). Never emit a generic, label-only greeting.
- One offer only. Ground every claim in the live state; never invent balances, counts, documents, tasks, or status. If the live state says a surface is unavailable, acknowledge that instead of fabricating a value.
- Use delivery "chat" for a visible suggestion in the current view; use "notify" for useful but low-urgency offers that should land quietly outside chat.
Respond as JSON: {"comment": <a short scoped greeting, or null>, "delivery": "chat" | "notify", "confidence": 0..1, "urgency": "low" | "medium" | "high", "title": <optional notification title>}.
The user just opened the "Transcripts" view.
Purpose: Recorded voice transcripts — play, scrub, and read
Declared intent: Offer to summarize or extract action items from the most recent voice transcripts, grounded in the recent-transcript count.
Live view state:
Live transcript state (last 7 days): 2 recorded transcripts.
Newest transcript: 0 minute(s) ago.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RAW MODEL OUTPUT:
{
  "comment": "I see you have 2 recent transcripts, with the newest just recorded—would you like a summary or key action items from them?",
  "delivery": "chat",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "urgency": "medium",
  "title": null
}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PARSED GREETING: {"text":"I see you have 2 recent transcripts, with the newest just recorded—would you like a summary or key action items from them?","delivery":"chat"}

==============================================================================
CASE: wallet (intent + representative live-state brief)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROMPT:
The user just navigated in the app. Decide whether to greet them with ONE specific, helpful, anticipatory offer scoped to where they just landed.
Rules:
- When the view below has a DECLARED INTENT, produce a single short greeting that fulfills that intent and references the live view state provided (concrete counts, names, addresses, readiness). This is expected and wanted — return it with high confidence (>= 0.8). Do NOT stay silent just because it is a settings or config screen; the declared intent is your mandate.
- When the view has NO declared intent, only offer something if it is clearly and specifically useful; otherwise stay silent (return null). Never emit a generic, label-only greeting.
- One offer only. Ground every claim in the live state; never invent balances, counts, documents, tasks, or status. If the live state says a surface is unavailable, acknowledge that instead of fabricating a value.
- Use delivery "chat" for a visible suggestion in the current view; use "notify" for useful but low-urgency offers that should land quietly outside chat.
Respond as JSON: {"comment": <a short scoped greeting, or null>, "delivery": "chat" | "notify", "confidence": 0..1, "urgency": "low" | "medium" | "high", "title": <optional notification title>}.
The user just opened the "Wallet" view.
Purpose: Token inventory, NFTs, LP positions, balance, and P&L
Declared intent: Offer a portfolio summary and a fund/swap next step, grounded in balances and readiness.
Live view state:
Live wallet state:
- Wallet source: env
- EVM address: 0x1234...abcd
- Readiness: EVM balances ready, execution ready
- Token inventory: 2 assets.
  - 0.42 ETH
  - 150.0 USDC
- 24h activity: 3 swaps, realized P&L 0.05 BNB, volume 1.2 BNB.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RAW MODEL OUTPUT:
{
  "comment": "Your wallet shows 0.42 ETH and 150 USDC, and you're ready to trade—would you like to swap some USDC for ETH now?",
  "delivery": "chat",
  "confidence": 0.94,
  "urgency": "medium",
  "title": null
}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PARSED GREETING: {"text":"Your wallet shows 0.42 ETH and 150 USDC, and you're ready to trade—would you like to swap some USDC for ETH now?","delivery":"chat"}

==============================================================================
CASE: database (NO intent — must be allowed to stay silent)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROMPT:
The user just navigated in the app. Decide whether to greet them with ONE specific, helpful, anticipatory offer scoped to where they just landed.
Rules:
- When the view below has a DECLARED INTENT, produce a single short greeting that fulfills that intent and references the live view state provided (concrete counts, names, addresses, readiness). This is expected and wanted — return it with high confidence (>= 0.8). Do NOT stay silent just because it is a settings or config screen; the declared intent is your mandate.
- When the view has NO declared intent, only offer something if it is clearly and specifically useful; otherwise stay silent (return null). Never emit a generic, label-only greeting.
- One offer only. Ground every claim in the live state; never invent balances, counts, documents, tasks, or status. If the live state says a surface is unavailable, acknowledge that instead of fabricating a value.
- Use delivery "chat" for a visible suggestion in the current view; use "notify" for useful but low-urgency offers that should land quietly outside chat.
Respond as JSON: {"comment": <a short scoped greeting, or null>, "delivery": "chat" | "notify", "confidence": 0..1, "urgency": "low" | "medium" | "high", "title": <optional notification title>}.
The user just opened the "Database" view.
Declared intent: none — this view has no declared anticipatory intent, so stay silent unless something is clearly useful.
Live view state: none available for this view.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RAW MODEL OUTPUT:
{
  "comment": null,
  "delivery": "notify",
  "confidence": 0.99,
  "urgency": "low"
}
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PARSED GREETING: null (stayed silent)

done
